name: SOC-001 | New Local User Account Created
description: Detects when a new local user account is created on a Windows system. Attackers often create backdoor accounts for persistence. Maps to MITRE ATT&CK T1136.001 (Create Account: Local Account).

qradar_rule.rule_name: SOC-001 | New Local User Account Created
qradar_rule.notes: Attacker perspective: After gaining initial access, attackers create local accounts to maintain persistence even if initial credentials are changed. Detection: Windows Event ID 4720 is generated whenever a new user account is created.

conditions.aql: SELECT * FROM events WHERE LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%' AND eventid = '4720' AND username NOT IN (SELECT username FROM reference_data.sets['SOC-Whitelisted-Admins']) LAST 60 MINUTES

tests.description: Windows Security Event: A user account was created
Must come from Windows event log source

offense_settings.message: ALERT: New local user account created on host [destinationIP] by [userName]. Possible backdoor account creation. Event ID: 4720. Review immediately.
offense_settings.description: A new local Windows user account was created. This may indicate attacker persistence via local account creation (MITRE T1136.001).

exclusions: Exclude if source username is in 'SOC-Whitelisted-Admins' reference set
Exclude if event occurs during scheduled maintenance windows
response_actions: Immediately disable the newly created account
Investigate source IP for signs of compromise
Check for other suspicious activity from same source
